metadata.title: Module 1: JavaScript Basics Quiz
metadata.description: Test your knowledge of JavaScript fundamentals

questions.question: What is the correct way to declare a variable in JavaScript?
questions.explanation: The 'var', 'let', and 'const' keywords are used to declare variables in JavaScript. 'var' was the original method, though 'let' and 'const' are preferred in modern JavaScript.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a JavaScript data type?
questions.explanation: JavaScript has: String, Number, Boolean, null, undefined, Symbol, and Object. There is no 'Character' type - JavaScript uses strings instead.

questions.question: JavaScript is a strongly typed language.
questions.explanation: JavaScript is dynamically typed. Types are determined at runtime and can change. Unlike languages such as Java or C, you don't need to explicitly declare the data type.

questions.question: Write a function that adds two numbers together.
acceptableAnswers: function add(a, b) { return a + b; }
function add(a, b) {\n  return a + b;\n}
const add = function(a, b) { return a + b; }
questions.explanation: Functions can be declared using 'function' keyword or as arrow functions. Both syntaxes are valid.

questions.question: Match the method to its description

pairs.right: Transform each element in an array
Remove elements that don't match a condition
Combine all array elements into one value

correctMatches.right: Transform each element in an array
Remove elements that don't match a condition
Combine all array elements into one value
